# Viability Torus Lab

Viability Torus Lab is a deterministic, seeded simulator for synthetic two-phase toroidal viability dynamics. Results demonstrate model behavior; they are not empirical evidence or operational policy recommendations.

## Machine interfaces

- Model manifest: /api/v1/model
- Complete five-layer laboratory registry: /api/v1/laboratory
- Maintenance patterns: /api/v1/maintenance-patterns
- v1 terminology alias: /api/v1/system-templates
- Published bounded-system instances and resolved protocols: /api/v1/systems
- Reusable scenario condition modules: /api/v1/scenario-modules
- Reusable intervention mechanisms and plans: /api/v1/interventions
- Scenario catalog compatibility alias: /api/v1/scenarios
- Run simulation or ensemble: POST /api/v1/simulate
- Compare experiments: POST /api/v1/compare
- Sweep parameters: POST /api/v1/sweep
- Validate a draft scenario: POST /api/v1/proposals/validate
- Reproduce a versioned paper fixture: GET /api/v1/research/paper?case=stable-periodic
- Analyze external mismatch telemetry: POST /api/v1/telemetry
- Browser UI Empirical Lab: local multi-column CSV mapping, gated observed-driver replay, and redacted evidence export (data remains in the browser)
- Opt-in researcher API: POST /api/v1/empirical/analyze (disabled by default; self-hosted bearer token, explicit remote-processing consent, request-only retention)
- Browser UI Evidence Registry: import redacted receipts, inspect anchor-relative compatibility, preserve negative studies, and summarize only compatible observed receipts
- Opt-in receipt aggregation API: POST /api/v1/empirical/aggregate (same disabled-by-default bearer token; redacted receipts only; descriptive, not meta-analysis)
- Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint: /mcp
- JSON Schema index: /schemas/v1/index.json
- Service description: /.well-known/viability-torus-lab.json
- Research paper: /paper.pdf
- Source and CLI: https://github.com/mindbomber/viability-torus-lab

Read /api/v1/model before running a large experiment. Compose inputs in this order: maintenance pattern, bounded-system instance, scenario module, intervention plan, then parameter overrides or custom events; interpret status only as the resulting run assessment. Treat domain and dynamic traits as separate descriptive metadata, not calibration selectors. A published default is a dated, low-confidence present-state hypothesis: inspect currentStateEstimate.asOfDate, confidence, observationWindow, candidateTimeAnchor, and parameterProxies before interpreting it. A scenario changes exogenous conditions while an intervention is a timed operator action. Use `systemId`, pass either an attached protocol id or reusable scenario-module id as `protocolId`, and select `interventionPlanId` (default `no-action`). Public requests are bounded by internal integration work as well as returned data. Prefer seeded ensembles over a single seed, retain experimentId and the evidence receipt, report maintenance-pattern/system/protocol/plan/model/contract versions, and preserve the synthetic-evidence disclaimer. Treat viability-boundary crossing, recoverable excursion, and irreversible rupture as distinct states. Inspect correctionMargin (C-D), debtAdjustedMargin (C-D-chi*debt), and radialVelocity (d-rho/dt) separately. Viability and phase regime are independent; do not interpret estimatedPhi when phase.identifiable is false. Imported mismatch telemetry tests an observable estimator but does not establish torus topology or domain validity. Empirical receipts are observed-descriptive and provisional; explanations are model attribution, not causal identification, parameter fitting, or validation of the theory. Evidence aggregation is anchor-relative and descriptive: preserve negative and non-comparable studies, exclude synthetic receipts, never average watchlist tiers, and treat non-combinability as a valid result. Local file MCP access is restricted to VTL_EMPIRICAL_ROOTS. Remote empirical tools require the configured bearer token and privacy.remoteProcessingAuthorized=true for analysis. Proposal validation never publishes a system or protocol; publication requires an explicit evidence declaration, human-reviewed source change, and deterministic reference tests.
